benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Not Taxed
description: VA disability compensation is excluded from federal adjusted gross income under federal law, and Ohio's individual income tax is computed starting from federal AGI, so VA disability compensation is not subject to Ohio income tax.
value_description: Fully tax-exempt

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Enhanced Homestead Exemption
description: Honorably discharged veterans rated 100% totally disabled by the VA (including total disability based on individual unemployability) receive an enhanced homestead exemption that removes a fixed indexed amount of their home's market value from property taxation, with NO age or income limit. Apply via county auditor with DD214 and VA award letter showing the 100% rating. A surviving spouse may continue the benefit. SB92 (pending, not yet law) would raise this to a full exemption of the entire homestead value. — VERIFIED: Disabled Veterans Enhanced Homestead Exemption: rating threshold and structure in the research are CORRECT (honorably discharged, VA 100% total disability OR 100% via individual unemployability/TDIU; no age or income limit; surviving spouse may continue). The DTE 105I form confirms the applicant 'must be 100% disabled by or be receiving 100% compensation for service-connected injuries.' However, the exemption DOLLAR AMOUNT in the research (stated base $50,000, 'reported ~$52,300-$56,000') is OUTDATED. The current indexed amount for tax year 2025 is $58,000 of market value (per Ohio Legislative Service Commission documents). Update the indexed figure to $58,000 (TY2025).
value_description: Indexed enhanced exemption (statutory base $50,000 of market value; reported ~$52,300-$56,000 in recent years due to annual indexing — confirm current-year amount with county auditor)

benefit_name: Free Hunting & Fishing Licenses for Disabled Veterans / Former POWs
description: Resident veterans determined by the VA to be permanently and totally disabled (and receiving VA pension/compensation), and former POWs, receive a fishing license, hunting license, fur taker permit, deer/turkey permits, and Wetlands Habitat Stamp free of charge. Approved for a 5-year cycle (licenses re-issued each license year). Veterans holding a wheelchair-symbol veteran plate are also eligible. Apply via Form DNR 9032 / 1-800-WILDLIFE.
value_description: Free hunting/fishing/trapping licenses and permits

benefit_name: Veterans with Disabilities License Plate - Registration Fee Exemption
description: Veterans with a service-connected disability rated or compensated at 100% by the VA, or who received a VA automobile grant, may obtain Veterans with Disabilities (or Purple Heart Disability) plates with no registration fee, no service fee, no local motor vehicle tax, and no transfer fees. Plates with the wheelchair symbol also grant accessible-parking privileges.
value_description: Full registration/tax/fee exemption on one vehicle

benefit_name: Ohio Veterans Homes (Sandusky & Georgetown)
description: Two state veterans homes provide standard nursing care, memory care, and skilled care; the Sandusky campus also offers domiciliary (independent/limited-supervised living). Eligibility: Ohio resident/citizen for at least one year and honorable discharge or separation under honorable conditions.
value_description: Long-term, skilled, memory, and domiciliary care

benefit_name: Heroes Promise Scholarship (Children of KIA Service Members)
description: Created by HB 1343 (effective July 1, 2011), provides an in-state tuition scholarship/waiver to children of Oklahoma military personnel killed in action after January 1, 2000, for up to 48 months. Recipients are exempt from Oklahoma's Promise high-school academic and family-income requirements. Administered by the Oklahoma State Regents for Higher Education.
value_description: Tuition scholarship up to 48 months (in-state tuition)

benefit_name: State Employment Veterans Preference
description: Honorably discharged veterans receive 5 preference points added to state employment exam scores; war veterans with a service-connected disability receive 10 points. 'Absolute Preference' veterans (30%+ disability rating) are placed at the top of certified hiring lists, and special disabled veterans (30%+) may bypass entrance exams. Direct-hire authority exists for nursing positions.
value_description: 5-point preference (10-point for disabled); absolute preference and exam waiver at 30%+

benefit_name: Sales Tax Exemption for 100% Disabled Veterans
description: Oklahoma residents certified by the USDVA as receiving service-connected compensation at the 100% rate for a permanent disability may obtain a sales tax exemption permit on purchases of tangible personal property and services. The exemption is capped at $25,000 of qualifying sales per year. A surviving spouse (not remarried) is capped at $1,000 per year.
value_description: Sales tax exempt up to $25,000/year of purchases ($1,000/year for surviving spouse)

benefit_name: Active Duty Military Pay Deduction
description: 100% of income received as salary or compensation by an Oklahoma resident serving in any component of the U.S. Armed Forces is deductible from Oklahoma taxable income. (Separately, VA disability compensation is not taxable federally and is therefore not included in Oklahoma taxable income; the state pages do not address VA disability comp as a distinct line item, but it is excluded by virtue of not being part of federal AGI.)
value_description: 100% of active-duty military pay deductible

benefit_name: Military Retirement Pay Income Tax Exemption (100%)
description: Beginning Tax Year 2022, Oklahoma residents may exclude 100% of retirement benefits received from any component of the U.S. Armed Forces from state income tax. This is a full exemption that replaced the older partial exclusion (greater of $10,000 or 75%). Confirmed by the Oklahoma Tax Commission income-tax help page.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt (100%, effective TY2022)

benefit_name: 100% Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Exemption
description: A head of household honorably discharged from the Armed Forces or Oklahoma National Guard and certified by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs as 100% permanently and totally disabled (service-connected) is exempt from ad valorem (property) tax on the FULL fair cash value of their homestead. The surviving spouse (if not remarried) also qualifies. Must own and occupy the homestead as of January 1. Applied via OTC Form 998 / Form 538-H with a current USDVA award letter.
value_description: Full exemption (100% of fair cash value of homestead)

benefit_name: Free State Park and Museum Admission
description: All honorably discharged veterans who are current Oklahoma residents receive free admission to all state-owned or state-operated parks and museums. Documentation required: valid Oklahoma driver license or state photo ID plus DD-214 or other honorable discharge evidence. No disability rating required.
value_description: Free admission to state parks and museums

benefit_name: Hunting/Fishing License Exemption for Disabled Veterans
description: Veterans with a 60% or greater service-connected disability are exempt from hunting and fishing license fees (free permit), or may purchase a Disability Lifetime Combination hunting/fishing license for $25. Veterans with LESS than 60% disability may purchase the Disability Lifetime Combination license for $200. 100% disabled veterans also receive fee exemptions for deer gun/archery/primitive firearms/bonus deer licenses.
value_description: Free license at 60%+; $25 lifetime combo at 60%+; $200 lifetime combo under 60%

benefit_name: Disabled American Veteran (DAV) License Plate
description: Veterans certified by the USDVA with a service-connected disability of at least 50% qualify for a Disabled American Veteran (DAV) license plate at a reduced registration rate of $5 plus applicable insurance and mailing fees. Applied via Service Oklahoma Form 751-J.
value_description: $5 registration (plus insurance/mailing fees); requires 50%+ service-connected disability

benefit_name: Free Driver License Issuance/Renewal for 100% Disabled Veterans
description: An honorably discharged veteran certified by the USDVA as a disabled veteran in receipt of compensation at the 100% rate for a permanent disability is not charged a fee for the issuance or renewal of an Oklahoma driver license.
value_description: Free driver license issuance/renewal

benefit_name: Oklahoma State Veterans Homes / Centers
description: ODVA operates state veterans homes (locations include Ardmore, Claremore, Clinton, Lawton, Norman, Sulphur, and Sallisaw) providing long-term skilled nursing care, federally approved by USDVA for per diem and licensed by the Oklahoma State Department of Health. Eligibility requires USDVA certification for per diem, at least 90 days active-duty service (exceptions for service-connected discharge), and a disability requiring intermediate/skilled nursing care. Admission is prioritized by oldest wartime period. Veterans with a 70% or greater service-connected rating receive FULL cost of care at no charge (effective 06-01-2009).
value_description: Full cost of care free for 70%+ service-connected veterans; otherwise per-diem/income-based

benefit_name: Oregon National Guard State Tuition Assistance (ONGSTA)
description: Provides 100% tuition funding (at no more than the state resident rate) for eligible Oregon Army and Air National Guard members at Oregon public universities and community colleges.
value_description: 100% of tuition at resident rate

benefit_name: Veterans Educational Bridge Grant (ODVA)
description: ODVA-administered grant for veterans who cannot complete their academic/training program on time due to unavailable required classes/training hours or a student-account debt blocking registration.
value_description: Up to $5,000

benefit_name: Veterans' Preference in Public Employment (ORS 408.225-408.237)
description: Public employers must grant a hiring/promotion preference to veterans and disabled veterans (and, effective Jan 1, 2026, current/former Oregon National Guard state servicemembers). On a 100-point scale, veterans receive 5 preference points and disabled veterans receive 10 points; qualifying veterans who meet minimum and special qualifications must generally be interviewed (ORS 408.237).
value_description: 5 points (veteran) / 10 points (disabled veteran) on a 100-point scale

benefit_name: ODVA Oregon Veteran Home Loan (ORVET)
description: State-run, below-market-rate home loan program exclusive to Oregon veterans (separate from the federal VA loan guaranty); one of only a handful of states offering this. Eligibility based on qualifying active-duty service (e.g. 178+ consecutive days post-1/31/1955 with honorable discharge, or service-connected disability/combat-zone criteria). ODVA sets rates and fees.
value_description: Below-market-rate mortgage (rate set by ODVA)

benefit_name: Federal pension subtraction (pre-Oct 1, 1991 military service)
description: Oregon does not have a general military-retirement exemption. Retirees may subtract the portion of taxable federal pension (including military retired pay) attributable to service before October 1, 1991: 100% if all service was before that date, proportional if service spans the date, 0% if all after. VA disability compensation is separately not taxed by Oregon.
value_description: 100% subtraction if all service pre-10/1/1991; proportional otherwise; no flat exemption for service after 10/1/1991

benefit_name: VA disability compensation not taxed
description: Oregon does not tax VA disability compensation; it is a tax-free monetary benefit and is excluded from Oregon taxable income.
value_description: Fully tax-exempt

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran or Surviving Spouse Property Tax Exemption (ORS 307.250)
description: Exempts a fixed portion of a disabled veteran's (or surviving spouse's) homestead assessed value. Veteran must be an Oregon resident who owns and lives in the home as primary residence, and be certified 40% or more disabled by the VA/a branch of service, OR certified 40%+ disabled annually by a licensed physician. Two tiers: the higher amount applies to service-connected disability; the lower amount applies to the 40%-or-more (non-service-connected/physician-certified) path. Amount increases 3% each year.
value_description: $32,512 of assessed value (service-connected disability) or $27,092 (40%+ disability, other than service-connected) per the current claim form (Rev. 11-07-25), applicable to property tax year beginning July 1, 2026

benefit_name: Free Disabled Veteran combination hunting/fishing/shellfish license (ODFW)
description: Service-connected disabled veterans rated at least 25% who have resided in Oregon at least 6 consecutive months before applying may receive a free combination license including hunting, fishing, shellfish, and the Columbia River Basin Endorsement (Ocean Endorsement added as of 2026). Requires VA letter showing 25%+ rating; a $2 shipping/handling fee applies.
value_description: Free combination license (hunting + fishing + shellfish + endorsements)

benefit_name: Oregon State Parks Special Access Pass for Veterans with Disabilities
description: Veterans with a service-connected disability can get a Special Access Pass giving free day-use parking and free camping in tent/RV/standard horse-camp sites, up to 4 nights at a time at a single park or 4 nights total per calendar month (standard rate applies beyond that). Pass also allows free camping on Oregon Dept. of Forestry lands up to 10 days/month. Pass valid 10 years.
value_description: Free day-use parking + free camping (4 nights at a time / 4 nights per calendar month)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran License Plates / one-time permanent registration
description: Service-connected disabled veterans qualifying as a 'disabled veteran' under ORS 805.100 may obtain disabled veteran plates with a one-time permanent vehicle registration (no recurring registration fee thereafter, aside from standard plate/replacement fees). Special veteran background plates (e.g. Purple Heart) are also available, some with surcharges.
value_description: One-time permanent registration (no recurring registration fee)

benefit_name: Oregon Veterans' Homes (Lebanon & The Dalles)
description: State-operated skilled-nursing/long-term care homes for honorably/generally discharged veterans, their spouses/surviving spouses, and Gold Star parents. Admission requires a physician's recommendation for skilled nursing care. Veterans rated 70% or more service-connected who need skilled nursing due to the disability may have their cost of care covered by the US VA.
value_description: Long-term/skilled nursing care; USVA may cover cost for 70%+ service-connected veterans

benefit_name: Educational Gratuity Program (EGP)
description: Provides financial assistance for higher education (college, trade school, apprenticeship in PA) to children of honorably discharged wartime veterans who have a service-connected TOTAL and PERMANENT (100%) disability, or whose veteran parent died in service during a period of war/armed conflict. Child must be age 16-23, a PA resident for 5 years prior, attend a PA school, and demonstrate financial need. Up to $500 per term for up to 8 terms ($4,000 total) per child.
value_description: Up to $500 per semester/term, max 8 terms ($4,000 total) per child

benefit_name: Veterans' Preference (Civil Service / Public Employment)
description: Under the PA Veterans' Preference Act (51 Pa.C.S. §§7101-7111), a qualifying veteran who passes a civil service appointment examination for a Commonwealth position receives an additional 10 points added to the final examination score for hiring eligibility. Preference applies to initial appointments, not to promotions or internal postings.
value_description: 10 points added to passing civil service exam score (initial hiring)

benefit_name: Amputee and Paralyzed Veterans Pension
description: A monthly state pension of $150 for veterans with a service-connected injury resulting in the loss or loss of use of two or more extremities, or paralysis (paraplegia/hemiplegia/quadriplegia), incurred during military service. Administered by DMVA; apply through the County Director of Veterans Affairs.
value_description: $150 per month

benefit_name: Blind Veterans Pension
description: A monthly state pension of $150 for veterans who are blind as a result of (or with blindness existing during) wartime military service. Administered by DMVA; apply through the County Director of Veterans Affairs.
value_description: $150 per month

benefit_name: Military Family Relief Assistance Program (MFRAP)
description: Grant assistance for PA-resident service members (and certain family members) who serve 30+ consecutive days of active duty and have a direct and immediate financial need resulting from circumstances beyond their control. Administered by DMVA.
value_description: Grant (need-based; amount set by DMVA)

benefit_name: Veterans Temporary Assistance (VTA)
description: Provides temporary financial assistance of up to $1,600 in a 12-month period to PA-resident veterans and their beneficiaries for the necessities of life (food, shelter, fuel, clothing) during a financial hardship. Administered by DMVA; apply through the County Director of Veterans Affairs.
value_description: Up to $1,600 per 12-month period

benefit_name: Military Retirement Pay Exemption (PA Personal Income Tax)
description: Pennsylvania does not tax military retirement pay. Per the PA Personal Income Tax Guide, retired/retainer pay of a member or former member of a uniformed service calculated under Chapter 71 of Title 10 U.S.C. is exempt from PA PIT. PA also does not tax VA disability compensation. No rating threshold applies.
value_description: Military retirement fully exempt from PA income tax; VA disability compensation not taxed

benefit_name: Disabled Veterans' Real Estate Tax Exemption
description: Full exemption from real estate (property) tax on a veteran's principal dwelling. Requires an honorable discharge, a 100% PERMANENT and total service-connected disability rating from the VA (or blindness, paraplegia, or loss of two or more limbs from military service), wartime service, PA residency, and a demonstrated financial need. As of Jan 1, 2025 the presumptive need (income) level was raised to $114,637, and a new law excludes all veterans benefits from the income assessment. Surviving spouses may continue to qualify. Apply through the County Director of Veterans Affairs.
value_description: Full exemption of real estate tax on principal residence (presumptive income/need level $114,637 as of 1/1/2025)

benefit_name: Free / Reduced Fishing License for Disabled Veterans
description: The PA Fish and Boat Commission issues a free fishing license to veterans with a 100% service-connected disability (documented by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs); a reduced-fee license is available at lower disability levels. A Combination Trout/Lake Erie Permit is issued free to those who qualify for the free license. Obtained through county treasurers, the Fish & Boat Commission Harrisburg office, or regional offices.
value_description: Free fishing license (100% service-connected) plus free Trout/Lake Erie combo permit; reduced-fee at lower ratings

benefit_name: Free Resident Disabled Veteran Hunting & Furtaker License
description: Pennsylvania issues a free Resident Disabled Veteran (DV) hunting and furtaker license to veterans with a 100% service-connected disability (VA certification of 100% physical disability or 100%/total compensation). The disability must be service-related and incurred during a war or armed conflict. Issued by county treasurers, PA Game Commission offices, and online.
value_description: Free hunting & furtaker license (war/armed-conflict service-connected disability)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran / Severely Disabled Veteran Special Registration Plates
description: PennDOT issues Disabled Veteran and Severely Disabled Veteran special registration plates to qualifying veterans (application form MV-371DV). The Severely Disabled Veteran plate corresponds to a 100% service-connected disability certified by the VA. Provides distinctive plates and, for severely disabled veterans, ties to the free-registration benefit.
value_description: Special Disabled/Severely Disabled Veteran plates

benefit_name: Free Vehicle Registration for Severely Disabled Veterans
description: A severely disabled veteran with a 100% service-connected disability certified by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs may receive free registration for one qualifying passenger vehicle or light-duty truck. The veteran applies via PennDOT/DMV (form MV-145V). Note: standard license plate fees may still apply for specialty plates; the registration fee is what is waived.
value_description: Free vehicle registration for one qualifying vehicle (100% service-connected)

benefit_name: Pennsylvania State Veterans' Homes (6 locations)
description: DMVA operates six veterans' homes — Philadelphia, Scranton (Gino J. Merli), Hollidaysburg, Erie, Spring City (Southeastern), and Pittsburgh (Southwestern) — providing skilled nursing, personal care, and domiciliary care. Eligibility: honorably discharged veteran (or eligible spouse/surviving spouse) who was a bona fide PA resident with home of record in PA upon entry into service. Residents make monthly maintenance-fee payments based on ability to pay; admission is first-come, first-served. No minimum disability rating required.
value_description: Skilled nursing / personal / domiciliary care at 6 state homes; cost based on ability to pay

benefit_name: 50% tuition discount for veterans' spouses and dependents
description: Spouses, children, and dependents of veterans receive a 50% discount on tuition, fees, books, and required materials to complete a degree at UPR and public postsecondary institutions (undergraduate, technical-professional, and graduate/professional levels), plus admission priority. Children/surviving spouses of service members who died in service-related circumstances receive a TOTAL tuition exemption. Per Ley 203-2007 as amended by Ley 110-2024.
value_description: 50% tuition/fees/books discount (100% for survivors of those who died in service)

benefit_name: Free UPR tuition for veterans
description: Veterans receive free tuition (exencion de matricula) at the University of Puerto Rico under Ley 203-2007. Documentation requirements were simplified by Ley 110-2024.
value_description: Full tuition exemption at UPR

benefit_name: Veterans hiring preference + 10-point exam bonus
description: Ley 203-2007 grants veterans hiring preference in PR government employment when equal in academic, technical, or experience qualifications, and adds a bonus of 10 points or 10% on competitive civil service examinations.
value_description: Hiring preference + 10 points / 10% on exams

benefit_name: VA pension aid-and-attendance disregarded for PR public assistance
description: When applying for income-conditioned public assistance benefits in Puerto Rico, VA special monthly pensions for Aid and Attendance and Housebound are NOT counted as income for eligibility purposes, per the Carta de Derechos del Veterano (Ley 203-2007).
value_description: A&A / Housebound pension excluded from income for benefit eligibility

benefit_name: Additional personal income tax exemption for veterans ($1,500)
description: Veterans residing in PR receive an additional lifetime personal income tax deduction/exemption of $1,500 per year on the PR income tax return (deduccion vitalicia). Married couples where both spouses are veterans receive a combined $3,000. Granted by Ley 203-2007 (Carta de Derechos del Veterano).
value_description: $1,500 ($3,000 if both spouses are veterans)

benefit_name: Full income tax exemption on military retirement pay (Ley 191-2024)
description: Complete (100%) exemption from Puerto Rico income tax on military retirement pension for retired U.S. Armed Forces members who reside in PR and qualify as veterans under Ley 203-2007. Enacted by Ley 191-2024 amending Section 1033.18 of the PR Internal Revenue Code (Ley 1-2011); claimable starting tax year 2025. Provide DD-214 or 1099-R. Because PR residents file PR returns and generally owe no federal income tax on PR-source income, this is the operative tax treatment.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt (100%)

benefit_name: VA disability compensation not taxed
description: VA disability compensation is excluded from taxable income. Puerto Rico residents generally owe no federal income tax on PR-source income and PR follows the federal exclusion of VA compensation, so VA disability pay is not subject to PR income tax. Could not locate a single PR primary page stating this verbatim, so flagged medium confidence.
value_description: Not taxed

benefit_name: Full property tax exemption for totally disabled veterans
description: Veterans certified as totally disabled (incapacitado total) receive a COMPLETE exemption from CRIM property tax on their primary residence and up to 1,000 square meters of land in urban areas or 1 cuerda in rural areas, so long as it serves as the residence of the veteran or immediate family. Requires VA certification of disability percentage; apply via CRIM (Form AS-38). Unremarried surviving spouse continues the exemption.
value_description: Full exemption (home + up to 1,000 sq m urban / 1 cuerda rural)

benefit_name: Property tax exemption on first $50,000 of assessed value (50%+ disabled)
description: Veterans with a service-connected disability rating of 50% or greater receive a CRIM property tax exemption on the first $50,000 of the assessed value of their primary residence. Granted by Ley 203-2007. Requires VA disability certification.
value_description: $50,000 off assessed value

benefit_name: Property tax exemption up to $5,000 assessed value (all veterans)
description: All qualifying PR veterans (any rating) receive a permanent CRIM property tax exemption on their good-faith primary residence up to $5,000 of assessed value. Surviving spouse continues the benefit. Apply via CRIM Form AS-38 within 30 days of acquisition.
value_description: $5,000 off assessed value

benefit_name: 50% discount on annual vehicle registration (marbete)
description: Veterans receive a 50% discount on the annual vehicle registration sticker (marbete) fee through CESCO/DTOP, for one vehicle registered in the veteran's name per year. Apply at CESCO with the veteran special-plate application (Form DTOP-DIS-058) and required documentation.
value_description: 50% off annual marbete (one vehicle)

benefit_name: Special veteran/disabled-veteran license plates (tablilla)
description: Puerto Rico issues special veteran and disabled-veteran license plates (tablilla especial para veteranos) through DTOP/CESCO. Apply with Form DTOP-DIS-058 and supporting documentation (veteran status; disability certification for disabled-veteran plates).
value_description: Special veteran/disabled-veteran plates

benefit_name: Vehicle excise tax (arbitrios) exemption up to $5,000 for disabled veterans
description: Vehicles for the personal use of disabled veterans, provided by or with the assistance of the VA, are exempt from PR vehicle excise tax (arbitrios) up to a $5,000 value, conditioned on retaining the vehicle for a minimum period (6 years; proposed legislation in 2025 would adjust the retention period/cap). Requires a VA disability certification letter at CESCO.
value_description: Excise tax exempt up to $5,000 vehicle value

benefit_name: Casa del Veterano (Juana Diaz) state veterans home
description: Puerto Rico operates one state veterans home, Casa del Veterano in Juana Diaz, with 240 beds (120 skilled nursing + 120 domiciliary care). Eligible residents include honorably discharged veterans, spouses/widows of veterans, and Gold Star parents of veterans who died in combat. NASVH-affiliated. Admissions: (787) 837-6574.
value_description: 240-bed skilled nursing + domiciliary care home

benefit_name: Disabled Veterans Tuition Waiver
description: Free tuition at Rhode Island's public colleges and universities (CCRI, Rhode Island College, URI) for permanent RI-resident veterans with a 10-100% service-connected disability. Student must apply for and use available financial aid first.
value_description: Full tuition waiver at RI public colleges/universities

benefit_name: State Tuition Assistance Program
description: Veterans may take up to five tuition-free classes per fall or spring semester at Rhode Island's public colleges and universities through the state tuition assistance program. Separately, active-duty members and their spouses/dependents get in-state tuition rates at CCRI, RIC, and URI regardless of residency.
value_description: Up to 5 tuition-free classes per semester at RI public colleges

benefit_name: Veterans' hiring preference (civil service points)
description: Under RI Gen. Laws 36-4-19, veterans who pass a competitive state civil service entrance exam receive a 5-point credit added to their score; disabled veterans (war veteran with a VA service-connected disability) receive a 10-point credit. Applicant must achieve a passing score before the preference is applied.
value_description: 5 points (veteran) / 10 points (disabled veteran) on civil service exams

benefit_name: Military Service Pension Modification (military retirement subtraction)
description: Rhode Island lets military retirees subtract a percentage of military service pension benefits (included in federal AGI) from RI taxable income, with no age requirement. The subtraction is phasing up 20 points/year: 60% for TY2025, 80% for TY2026, and 100% (fully exempt) starting TY2027. — VERIFIED: Military service pensions are FULLY EXEMPT (100%) from Rhode Island Personal Income Tax for all tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2023 - there is NO phase-in. Under R.I. Gen. Laws 44-30-12(c)(11), a taxpayer subtracts the full amount of military service pension benefits included in federal AGI via Schedule M, with no age or income requirement. The research's claim of an 80%/20-points-per-year phase-in (80% for 2026, 100% in 2027) is incorrect; the official RI Division of Taxation Retirement Income Guide (Pub 2026-01, issued Feb 2026, Section 2) states military service pensions are fully exempt since TY2023. (Separately, VA disability compensation is also not taxed because RI starts from federal AGI, which excludes it.)
value_description: 80% exempt for tax year 2026; 100% (fully exempt) from tax year 2027

benefit_name: VA disability compensation - not taxed
description: VA disability compensation is excluded from federal adjusted gross income, and Rhode Island's personal income tax starts from federal AGI, so VA disability compensation is not subject to RI state income tax. RI's military tax page addresses combat-zone pay exclusion/conformity but does not add VA disability comp back to income.
value_description: Not taxed by Rhode Island

benefit_name: Veterans' property tax exemption (RI Gen. Laws sec. 44-3-4)
description: State statute sets a base exemption of $1,000 of assessed value for qualifying veterans (honorable discharge), with a $10,000 exemption for veterans the VA determines totally disabled through service-connected disability, and a $10,000 exemption for specially adapted housing. These are statutory minimums; each city/town may increase the dollar amounts (and partial-disability amounts) by local ordinance, so the actual exemption varies widely by municipality. The totally-disabled tier requires a VA total-disability determination (not necessarily permanent).
value_description: $1,000 base standard veteran; $10,000 for VA-rated totally disabled (service-connected); $10,000 specially adapted housing - municipalities may raise amounts by ordinance

benefit_name: Free hunting/fishing license for 100% disabled
description: Persons with a 100% permanent disability status (from VA or SSA) are issued a no-cost permanent RI freshwater fishing, hunting, or combo license through DEM, and are exempt from needing a saltwater fishing license (must carry the original VA decision document). Requires a current original VA letter on agency letterhead.
value_description: Free permanent hunting/fishing license; saltwater license exemption

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran license plate / registration fee waiver
description: Rhode Island issues a disabled veteran license plate at no cost. The DMV requires proof of service (DD-214 or equivalent) and a VA form indicating 100% disabled. The veteran designation can also be added free to an existing RI license or ID.
value_description: Free disabled veteran plate (no registration plate fee)

benefit_name: Veteran Disability Parking Placard
description: Veterans who are 100% service-connected disabled may obtain a veteran disability parking placard by submitting the Veteran Disability Parking Placard Application plus a VA war-related disabled declaration letter. Veterans below the 100% threshold may instead apply for a standard disability placard with physician certification.
value_description: Disabled parking placard

benefit_name: Rhode Island Veterans Home (Bristol)
description: State-operated 208-bed residential and skilled nursing care facility in Bristol for Rhode Island war veterans. Residents pay an assessment based on a percentage of their income for housing and care; it is not a Medicaid facility and no asset spend-down is required to qualify. Contact 401-253-8000.
value_description: Income-based assessment; 208-bed facility

benefit_name: Free Tuition for Certain War Veterans' Children
description: Children (age 26 or younger) of qualifying wartime veterans may attend any SC state-supported college, university, or technical school free of tuition. The veteran must have served honorably during a defined wartime period AND meet one of: killed in action, died of service-related causes, permanently and totally disabled (VA-determined), Medal of Honor or Purple Heart recipient, former POW, or MIA. Both veteran and student must meet SC residency requirements (generally 12 months). Covers tuition only — not room, board, or fees. Administered by SCDVA under SC Code Section 59-111-20.
value_description: Free tuition at SC state-supported institutions (tuition only)

benefit_name: State Employment Veterans Hiring Preference
description: South Carolina provides a veterans hiring preference in state employment: at least one qualified veteran must be interviewed for each full-time-equivalent position. The veteran must have served on active duty, meet minimum training/experience requirements, be able to perform the duties, and provide a DD-214 (no documentation = no preference). Administered per SC Code Title 25, Chapter 11.
value_description: Guaranteed interview for qualified veteran applicants

benefit_name: Military Retirement Pay Full Income Tax Exemption
description: All military retirement income is fully deductible from South Carolina taxable income with no age requirement and no income cap (SC Code Section 12-6-1171). Covers Armed Forces retirement and survivor plans (SBP/RCSBP/RSFPP). Full exemption has been in place since tax year 2022.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt (no cap, no age limit)

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Not Taxed
description: VA disability compensation and VA pension are not subject to South Carolina income tax. These payments are excluded from federal taxable income (the starting point for the SC return), so they are not taxed at the state level. This is treatment, not a fixed dollar benefit.
value_description: VA disability compensation fully exempt from state income tax

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption (home + 5 acres)
description: Full exemption from property tax on a home and the land it sits on (up to 5 acres, same parcel, owner-occupied, titled solely to the veteran or jointly with spouse) for veterans with a total, permanent, service-connected disability as determined by the VA. As of a recent expansion (retroactive to 2022), the exemption applies beginning with the year the disability occurs, not just the application year. Apply via MyDORWAY or through a County Veterans Service Officer with VA documentation of 100% permanent and total service-connected disability.
value_description: Full exemption (100% of property tax on home + up to 5 acres)

benefit_name: Free/Reduced Disability Hunting & Fishing License
description: Resident veterans with a VA service-connected disability rating who are eligible for compensation may obtain a free 3-year Disability Combination License (freshwater + saltwater fishing, state wildlife hunting, base deer tags) from SCDNR. Requires a copy of unexpired SC driver's license/ID and current VA disability certification dated within the last 12 months.
value_description: Free 3-year disability hunting/fishing combination license

benefit_name: Free Disabled Veteran License Plate
description: Veterans classified as totally and permanently disabled due to service-connected disabilities (or entitled to compensation for loss/loss of use of limbs or virtual blindness) qualify for a free Disabled Veteran license plate (also available as Handicapped and Motorcycle variants) with no plate fee. Available by mail only via SCDMV HQ in Blythewood using Application for Military License Plate (Form MV-37) with VA disability documentation.
value_description: No-fee Disabled Veteran plate

benefit_name: Property Tax Exemption on up to 2 Vehicles
description: Veterans with a total, permanent, service-connected disability are exempt from property tax on up to two private passenger vehicles owned or leased solely by the veteran or jointly with a spouse. Surviving spouses qualify, including those who relocate to SC after the veteran's death. Administered by SCDOR.
value_description: Full property-tax exemption on up to 2 vehicles

benefit_name: State Veterans Nursing Homes
description: South Carolina operates state veterans nursing-care homes providing skilled and intermediate nursing care, including Veterans' Victory House (Walterboro, 220 beds) and E. Roy Stone Jr. Veterans Pavilion (Columbia). Admission is open to veterans separated under general or honorable conditions who qualify as SC citizens and need skilled or intermediate nursing home care. Administered by SCDVA. Specific per-diem cost not confirmed from a primary .gov source.
value_description: Skilled/intermediate nursing care (cost not confirmed)

benefit_name: Free Tuition for Children of Deceased / POW / MIA Service Members
description: Children under age 25 who are SD residents and whose parent was killed in action or died while on active duty (parent must have been an SD resident at least 6 months before service) receive free tuition at state-supported schools. Spouses and children of POW/MIA service members receive tuition and mandatory-fee waivers for up to eight semesters, provided they lack equal or greater federal benefits.
value_description: Free tuition and mandatory fees (up to 8 semesters for POW/MIA dependents)

benefit_name: Free Tuition for War-Service Veterans (Reduced/Free Tuition Program)
description: An honorably discharged South Dakota resident veteran who qualifies for in-state tuition and is NOT eligible for the GI Bill or other federal educational assistance may attend a state-supported university or technical college tuition-free. Benefit is one month of free tuition for each month of qualifying war service, ranging from one to four academic years maximum. Apply via the E-12 form through the SD Dept. of Veterans Affairs.
value_description: Free tuition; 1 month per qualifying service month, up to 4 academic years

benefit_name: National Guard 100% Tuition Reduction
description: Active members of the South Dakota National Guard receive a 100% reduction in tuition charges at state universities and technical colleges (up to 128 undergraduate credits and 32 graduate credits), contingent on maintaining Guard membership and meeting drill-attendance requirements. This is a Guard membership benefit rather than a veteran benefit.
value_description: 100% tuition reduction (up to 128 undergrad / 32 grad credits)

benefit_name: Veterans Employment Preference (SDCL 33A-2)
description: Honorably discharged veterans (and certain spouses/surviving spouses/family members who claim on the veteran's behalf) receive preference in public employment across state, county, city, and public school systems. Disabled veterans receive preference over non-disabled veterans. In the state executive branch, preference-eligible applicants who meet job qualifications are guaranteed an interview. The page describes federal 5/10-point scoring but does not state a specific point value for SD state/local positions.
value_description: Hiring preference; guaranteed interview in state executive branch (point value not specified for SD positions)

benefit_name: South Dakota Veterans Bonus
description: Cash bonus for veterans who were SD legal residents for at least six months immediately before active duty. Up to $500 for Persian Gulf-era service (Aug 2, 1990 - Dec 31, 1992) and up to an additional $500 for qualifying service after Jan 1, 1993 (including ongoing Global War on Terror service from Sept 11, 2001). Active duty for training does not count. Prior WWI/WWII/Korea/Vietnam bonus programs are closed.
value_description: Up to $500 per qualifying period (up to $1,000 total)

benefit_name: No State Income Tax on Military Retirement or VA Disability
description: South Dakota levies no individual income tax. Military retirement pay, VA disability compensation, and all other income are free of state income tax. No filing or exemption claim is required.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt (no state income tax exists)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption (SDCL 10-4-40)
description: Exempts a portion of the assessed value of an owner-occupied dwelling (house, garage, and up to one acre) for a veteran rated permanently AND totally disabled from a service-connected disability by the VA. The statute requires a permanent and total (P&T) service-connected rating; it does not key off a numeric percentage other than total/100%. Apply on a form prescribed by the secretary of revenue to the county Director of Equalization by November 1 for the following assessment year.
value_description: $200,000 of full-and-true (assessed) value exempted

benefit_name: Paraplegic / Wheelchair Home Full Property Tax Exemption (SDCL 10-4-24.10)
description: Dwellings specifically designed for wheelchair use, owned and occupied by a veteran with the loss or loss of use of both lower extremities (paraplegic), are FULLY exempt from property taxation (no dollar cap). Also covers the unremarried widow/widower of such a veteran. Property must be owned and occupied for one full calendar year before the exemption takes effect; apply to the county assessor by November 1.
value_description: Full exemption (no dollar cap)

benefit_name: Surviving Spouse Property Tax Exemption (SDCL 10-4-41)
description: Unremarried surviving spouse of a veteran who was permanently and totally disabled from a service-connected disability, OR whose death was service-connected (spouse receiving VA Dependency and Indemnity Compensation), receives a partial exemption on an owner-occupied dwelling. Apply to the county by November 1.
value_description: $150,000 of full-and-true (assessed) value exempted

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran / POW Reduced-Fee Hunting & Fishing License
description: Any South Dakota RESIDENT veteran who has received the VA 'K' award OR receives a VA allotment for a service-connected disability rated 40% or greater may purchase a reduced-fee combination hunting and fishing license. A $10 application fee applies and the license is valid for four years. Authorized under SDCL 41-6-10.2 and 41-17-13.4.
value_description: Reduced-fee combination hunting/fishing license; valid 4 years

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Lifetime State Park Entrance License
description: Resident veterans deemed 100% disabled receive a free LIFETIME state park entrance license (free vehicle entry to all SD state parks/recreation areas), plus a 50% discount on camping fees and associated electrical-service fees. Apply online through GFP and upload VA verification; approved applicants receive a billfold card.
value_description: Free lifetime park entry + 50% off camping fees

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran License Plate (Registration Fee Waived)
description: Resident veterans who hold a VA 'K' award, received an automobile under Public Law 187, hold a statutory benefit for loss/loss of use of one or more extremities, or receive a VA allotment for total service-connected disability may obtain a Disabled Veteran plate. The injury must have been incurred on active duty during wartime or armed conflict. The annual vehicle REGISTRATION fee is waived; only a $10 annual plate charge applies.
value_description: Registration fee waived; $10 annual plate fee only

benefit_name: Purple Heart and Medal of Honor Plates (Fee Waived)
description: Purple Heart plates carry no registration fee (only a $10 annual plate charge). Medal of Honor plates carry no fee at all. Other valor-medal plates (Silver Star, Bronze Star, Navy Cross, etc.) require both the registration fee and the $10 plate fee at initial application.
value_description: Registration fee waived (Purple Heart: $10 plate fee; Medal of Honor: no fee)

benefit_name: Michael J. Fitzmaurice State Veterans Home (Hot Springs)
description: 100-bed state veterans home (78 nursing beds, 24 domiciliary/assisted-living beds) for eligible SD veterans and their spouses/widows/widowers. Eligibility tied to qualifying for VA per-diem domiciliary or nursing-home care; veterans domiciled in SD within the past five years receive admission preference. Costs (subject to change): assisted living about $203.75/day or 50% of a single resident's income (55% of combined income for couples); nursing-facility care about $360.00/day.
value_description: Assisted living ~$203.75/day (or 50% of income); nursing care ~$360.00/day

benefit_name: Helping Heroes Grant
description: Lottery-funded higher-education grant for honorably discharged veterans (and current/former Reserve or TN National Guard members) who received the Iraq Campaign Medal, Afghanistan Campaign Medal, or Global War on Terrorism Expeditionary Medal. Awarded first-come to roughly 375 veterans annually, up to 8 full semesters. Administered by the TN Student Assistance Corporation.
value_description: $1,000 per semester (12+ semester hours); $500 per semester (6-11 hours)

benefit_name: Veterans' Preference in State Employment (TEAM Act)
description: Mandatory hiring/interview preference for honorably discharged veterans (and eligible spouses/surviving spouses) in TN state service. When a veteran and a non-veteran candidate have equal qualifications, the veteran must be given preference; an appointing authority passing over an eligible veteran must file written reasons within 30 days. Per TCA 8-30-307.
value_description: Tie-breaker hiring preference + interview preference

benefit_name: No State Income Tax on Military Retirement / VA Compensation
description: Tennessee levies no state income tax on wages or retirement income. The Hall Income Tax (interest/dividends only) was fully repealed effective Jan 1, 2021. Military retirement pay and VA disability compensation are not taxed by the state.
value_description: Military retirement fully untaxed; VA disability compensation untaxed (no state income tax)

benefit_name: Veteran ID Designation on Driver License
description: Tennessee offers a 'VETERAN' designation/ID printed on a state driver license or ID card for honorably discharged veterans, used to verify status for benefits and discounts.
value_description: Veteran designation on license/ID

benefit_name: Property Tax Relief for Disabled Veterans
description: State-funded reimbursement of property taxes paid on a disabled veteran's primary residence, administered through the County Trustee. Eligibility requires a service-connected PERMANENT AND TOTAL disability as determined by the VA, OR a qualifying condition (paraplegia/permanent paralysis of both legs, loss or loss of use of two or more limbs, legal blindness), OR a 100% total and permanent rating as a former POW. Veteran must own and occupy the home.
value_description: Reimbursement of property tax calculated on up to $175,000 of the home's full market value

benefit_name: Property Tax Relief for Surviving Spouses (of disabled veterans)
description: Surviving spouse of a qualifying disabled veteran may continue to receive the property tax relief benefit. Detailed eligibility/amount is administered through the County Trustee; the state page lists it as a distinct program but does not republish the dollar figures, so the specific cap is confirmed only by reference to the disabled-veteran program ($175,000 market-value basis).
value_description: Continuation of disabled-veteran property tax relief (market-value basis up to $175,000 per the linked program)

benefit_name: Lifetime Sport Hunting & Fishing License (Disabled Veterans)
description: Veterans who are 100% permanently and totally disabled from service-connected causes, OR 30% or more disabled by reason of service in any war, may obtain a combined lifetime sport fishing and hunting license. Issued via TWRA Miscellaneous Licenses Application.
value_description: One-time $10 fee for lifetime sport fishing + hunting license

benefit_name: State Park Camping & Lodging Discount
description: Tennessee resident veterans get 50% off standard nightly camping base rates during off-season (Dec 1-Feb 28/29). Veterans with a 100% total service-connected disability get 50% off camping AND overnight cabin lodging fees (premium sites excluded). Proof of service and TN residency required.
value_description: 50% off camping (off-season); 50% off camping + cabin lodging for 100% disabled

benefit_name: Free Disabled Driver Decal
description: Disabled veterans who meet the state's disabled-driver definition (TCA 55-21-102) may obtain a free disabled-driver decal to affix to their existing specialty plate. Eligibility follows the general disabled-driver statute rather than a specific VA percentage.
value_description: Free disabled decal

benefit_name: Free Disabled Veteran License Plate & Registration
description: A veteran with a VA-determined 100% permanent total service-connected disability receives one license plate and registration free of charge. Also exempts 100% P&T veterans and former POWs from the county motor vehicle privilege tax. Per TCA 55-4-256; administered by TN Dept of Revenue (VR-13).
value_description: One free plate + free registration; county wheel/privilege tax exemption

benefit_name: Tennessee State Veterans Homes
description: Five state veterans homes provide long-term skilled nursing/residential care for eligible veterans, operated by the Tennessee State Veterans Home Board. Locations: Humboldt, Murfreesboro, Clarksville, Knoxville, and Cleveland. Per-facility eligibility/services available on each home's site.
value_description: 5 homes (Humboldt, Murfreesboro, Clarksville, Knoxville, Cleveland)

benefit_name: Hazlewood Act Tuition Exemption
description: Provides up to 150 credit hours of tuition and most fee charges exempted at Texas public colleges/universities. Does NOT cover living expenses, books, or supply fees. Veteran must have entered service in Texas (or been a Texas resident), served at least 181 days active duty, and received an honorable or under-honorable-conditions discharge, and have exhausted federal education benefits. Unused hours can transfer to a dependent child age 25 or younger (Hazlewood Legacy). Spouses/dependents of veterans who are 100% permanent & total service-connected, or who were KIA/MIA/died of service-connected causes, may receive their own 150 hours.
value_description: Up to 150 semester credit hours tuition exemption at TX public institutions

benefit_name: Veteran's Employment Preference (state jobs)
description: Under Texas Government Code Chapter 657, qualified veterans, surviving spouses of veterans who have not remarried, certain spouses of veterans (where spouse is primary household income and veteran is totally disabled), and orphans of veterans are entitled to a hiring preference over non-veterans for state agency positions. State agencies also maintain a veteran's employment preference interview/hiring framework. No VA disability rating is required for the basic preference.
value_description: Hiring preference over equally qualified non-veterans for state employment

benefit_name: Texas Veterans Land Board (VLB) Loans — Land, Home, and Home Improvement
description: The VLB offers below-market-rate loans to qualified Texas veterans/military members: land loans, home loans, and home improvement loans (up to $50,000, 2-20 year term). Veterans with a VA service-connected disability of 30% or greater qualify for a discounted interest rate (e.g., home improvement base rate was 6.35% with a 5.85% disabled-veteran discounted rate as of the cited GLO page; rates adjust weekly). No down payment required for the home improvement program.
value_description: Below-market loans; extra rate discount for 30%+ disabled veterans

benefit_name: 100% Disabled Veteran / P&T Residence Homestead Exemption (total exemption)
description: Veterans who receive 100% disability compensation from the VA — either via a 100% schedular rating OR a determination of individual unemployability (TDIU) — are exempt from property tax on the TOTAL appraised value of their residence homestead. An unmarried surviving spouse who owned and occupied the home at the veteran's death keeps the exemption (if they move, the exemption is capped at the prior home's last-year dollar amount).
value_description: Full (100%) exemption of the residence homestead's appraised value

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption (tiered, 10-99%)
description: Veterans with a VA service-connected disability rating from 10% to less than 100% receive a flat dollar reduction off the value of any one property they own (not limited to homestead). Amount scales with rating tier. Veterans age 65+ with at least 10%, the totally blind, or those who lost use of a limb qualify for the top $12,000 amount regardless of tier.

benefit_name: Surviving Spouse of Service Member Killed/Fatally Injured in Line of Duty
description: An unmarried surviving spouse of a U.S. armed services member who was killed or fatally injured in the line of duty is entitled to a total property tax exemption on their residence homestead. The exemption transfers (subject to a dollar cap) if the spouse moves to a new homestead, as long as they have not remarried.
value_description: Total (100%) residence homestead exemption

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran 'Super Combo' Hunting & All-Water Fishing License (Free)
description: Free Super Combo package (hunting + all-water fishing license plus five state endorsements, excluding the reptile/amphibian endorsement) for resident or non-resident disabled veterans. Eligibility: loss of use of a foot or leg, OR a VA disability rating of 50% or more, while receiving federal compensation for the disability. Federal Duck Stamp not included. Available in person only at a license retailer with VA proof of disability rate or a TX driver license with 'Disabled Veteran' designation.
value_description: Free Super Combo hunting/fishing license

benefit_name: State Parks Parklands Passport (free entry)
description: TPWD offers free Texas state park entry passes for veterans. The Disabled Veterans Passport gives free entry to the veteran PLUS a companion, requiring a 60% or more service-connected disability or loss of a lower extremity. A separate free Veterans Passport gives free entry (pass holder only) to any honorably discharged U.S. veteran regardless of disability. A Gold Star Passport covers immediate family of those who died in service. Obtain at any state park with required documentation.
value_description: Free state park entry (Disabled Veterans Passport adds a companion)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran License Plates + Registration Fee Waiver
description: A qualifying disabled veteran may register ONE vehicle for personal use with all registration fees waived except the plate fee. The fee exemption requires at least a 50% service-connected disability, OR a 40% service-connected disability due to amputation of a lower extremity. The annual fee for the first set of Disabled Veteran plates is $3; additional sets are free. Plates may display the International Symbol of Access. Apply via Form VTR-615 through the county tax office.
value_description: Registration fees waived on one vehicle; $3 annual plate fee (first set)

benefit_name: Texas State Veterans Homes (VLB-operated long-term care)
description: The Texas Veterans Land Board (under the General Land Office) operates Texas State Veterans Homes offering long-term skilled nursing/memory care for veterans, their spouses, and Gold Star parents at locations including Bonham, Floresville, Houston, Temple, Tyler, Fort Worth, McAllen, Big Spring, El Paso, and Amarillo. Per GLO, eligible veterans with a 70% or higher service-connected disability may live at the home at no cost to them (the VA per diem covers the cost).
value_description: Long-term care; free for veterans rated 70%+ service-connected

benefit_name: Purple Heart Tuition Waiver
description: Recipients of the Purple Heart receive a tuition waiver at all Utah public institutions of higher education, usable toward a degree up to and including a Master's degree. The veteran submits proof of Purple Heart status to the state institution they attend.
value_description: Full tuition waiver (up to Master's degree) at Utah public colleges

benefit_name: Scott B. Lundell Military Survivors Tuition Waiver
description: Surviving dependents (spouse/children) of service members killed in the line of duty on or after September 11, 2001 receive a tuition waiver at Utah state higher-education institutions, allowing them to pursue a degree without paying tuition.
value_description: Full tuition waiver at Utah state schools for eligible survivors

benefit_name: Veterans Tuition Gap Program
description: State-funded grant for Utah-resident veterans in the final year of their first bachelor's degree who have exhausted federal veteran education benefits. Covers tuition, fees, books, education-related supplies, and a housing allowance, subject to available funding (an ongoing program funded around $125,000 annually per legislative budget records). Requires satisfactory academic progress and a completed FAFSA.
value_description: Grant covering tuition/fees/books/housing in final bachelor's year (subject to funding)

benefit_name: Recently Deployed Veteran Employment Tax Credit
description: Nonrefundable Utah income tax credit for employers who hire a qualified recently deployed veteran — a person mobilized to active federal military service who received an honorable or general discharge within the two-year period before employment begins. This is an employer-side hiring incentive rather than a benefit paid directly to the veteran.
value_description: Employer nonrefundable tax credit for hiring recently deployed veterans

benefit_name: Veterans Preference in State Hiring
description: Veterans' preference for initial employment with Utah state government and other public employers (Utah Code Title 71A-2, formerly Title 71-10). A qualifying veteran receives 5 points (or 5% of the total possible score, whichever is greater); a veteran with a service-connected disability or a Purple Heart recipient receives 10 points (or 10%, whichever is greater). Documentation such as DD214/NGB22 and a VA status letter is required; spouses may claim preference based on the veteran's service in certain cases.
value_description: 5 points (veteran) / 10 points (disabled vet or Purple Heart) on public-employment scoring

benefit_name: Military Retirement Income Tax Credit
description: Nonrefundable Utah income tax credit equal to 4.5% of the taxable military retirement pay included in adjusted gross income. Because Utah's flat income tax rate is also 4.5%, this credit fully offsets the state tax on military retirement pay, effectively exempting it. Covers retirement pay and survivor benefits tied to armed forces service including Reserve/National Guard. Excludes Social Security, 401(k)/IRA distributions, and non-military federal retirement. The credit can reduce tax to zero but is not refundable beyond tax owed.
value_description: Credit = 4.5% of taxable military retirement pay (offsets Utah's 4.5% flat tax = effectively fully exempt)

benefit_name: Nonresident Active Duty Military Pay Subtraction
description: Nonresident service members do not pay Utah income tax on active duty military pay; the active duty pay included in federal AGI is deducted on TC-40A Part 2 using code 82 (filed on the nonresident/part-year TC-40B). This benefits active duty members stationed in Utah who are domiciled elsewhere.
value_description: Active duty pay subtracted for nonresidents (code 82)

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Not Taxed
description: Utah does not tax VA disability compensation. Utah income tax begins with federal adjusted gross income, and VA disability compensation is excluded from federal gross income, so it is not subject to Utah income tax. No Utah-specific statute is needed; this follows from Utah's conformity to federal AGI. (No Utah .gov page explicitly itemizes VA disability comp as a subtraction, which is why confidence is medium rather than high.)
value_description: Not taxed by Utah

benefit_name: Utah State Veterans Cemetery (Camp Williams)
description: Burial benefits at the Utah State Veterans Cemetery for eligible veterans (active duty, wartime service, and reservists/National Guard with 20+ years of qualifying service) and their eligible family members (spouse and dependents).
value_description: State veterans cemetery burial eligibility

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Abatement (Exemption)
description: Property tax abatement for veterans with a service-connected disability of at least 10%, applied to the primary residence (up to 1 acre) and/or personal-use tangible property. The abatement equals the disability percentage multiplied by the statutory maximum; at 100% disability the maximum abatement of taxable value is $505,548 for 2026 (e.g., 10% x $505,548 = $50,554). A veteran rated as Individually Unemployable (IU) by the VA is treated as 100% disabled regardless of the scheduled percentage. Available to the disabled veteran, an unmarried surviving spouse, or minor orphans. Apply to the county by September 1. 100%/P&T is NOT required to qualify. — VERIFIED: Disabled Veteran Property Tax Abatement: requires a service-connected disability of at least 10% (P&T NOT required); abatement = disability % x the statutory maximum taxable value. For 2026 the maximum taxable value abated at 100% disability is $535,459 (NOT $505,548 - that is the prior-year/2025 figure carried over from the stale veterans.utah.gov FAQ). E.g., 10% x $535,459 = $53,546. IU (Individual Unemployability) is treated as 100%. Available to the veteran, unmarried surviving spouse, or minor orphans; apply to the county by September 1.
value_description: Disability % x $505,548 max taxable value abated (2026); 100% rating = $505,548

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Hunting / Fishing / Combination License Discount
description: Utah residents with a service-connected disability rating of 20% or greater may purchase discounted hunting, fishing, or combination licenses through the Division of Wildlife Resources upon furnishing VA/Armed Forces verification. Discounted hunting license is $25.50 (vs $34); combination license $28.50 (vs $38); fishing license $12. Approval carries forward — no need to reapply each year. These are discounts, not free licenses.
value_description: Hunting $25.50, Combination $28.50, Fishing $12 (discounted, not free)

benefit_name: Veterans with Disabilities Honor Pass (State Parks)
description: Free Utah State Parks access pass for Utah-resident veterans with an honorable discharge and a service-related disability who provide a current VA summary-of-benefits letter. The benefit is free. Certain parks are excluded (e.g., Flight Park, Piute, Jordan River OHV, Utahraptor, Lost Creek State Parks, and This Is The Place Heritage Park). A separate Adventure Pass pilot offers free day-use access to a limited number (1,600) of veterans holding an Interagency Military Lifetime Pass; no disability rating required for that pilot. The Honor Pass does not specify a numeric rating threshold beyond having a service-related disability.
value_description: Free state parks pass for disabled veterans (with park exclusions)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran License Plate
description: Special-group license plate for Utah-resident disabled veterans. Requires a letter from the Utah Dept. of Veterans and Military Affairs or other U.S. military entity certifying disabled status (no specific rating percentage published). Has NO initial or annual contribution/special-plate fee, but standard plate fee ($12.50), registration certificate ($4.00), and normal registration fees/taxes still apply. The plate does NOT itself grant handicapped parking (a separate placard is required) and does NOT provide a property tax or registration-fee exemption.
value_description: No special-plate contribution fee; standard registration fees still apply

benefit_name: Purple Heart / Combat Wounded License Plate
description: Special license plate for Purple Heart recipients. Unlike most special plates, it carries NO special plate fee, and vehicles with a Purple Heart plate are exempt from the $1.00/year fuel tax fee that applies to other vehicles. Issued to qualifying Purple Heart recipients through the Utah DMV.
value_description: No special plate fee; exempt from $1.00/yr fuel tax fee

benefit_name: Utah State Veterans Nursing Homes
description: Utah operates four state veterans homes providing skilled nursing care: William E. Christoffersen Salt Lake Veterans Home (Salt Lake City, ~81 beds), George E. Wahlen Ogden Veterans Home (Ogden, ~120 beds), Central Utah Veterans Home (Payson, ~108 beds), and Southern Utah Veterans Home (Ivins, ~108 beds). Eligible residents include wartime and peacetime veterans, qualifying spouses (with at least one year of marriage), and Gold Star parents.
value_description: 4 skilled-nursing homes (SLC, Ogden, Payson, Ivins)

benefit_name: Virginia Military Survivors and Dependents Education Program (VMSDEP)
description: Waives tuition and mandatory fees at Virginia public colleges/universities for spouses and children of service members who were killed, MIA, POW, or who became permanently and totally disabled (or rated at least 90% permanently disabled) due to qualifying military service. Up to 8 semesters (4 academic years). Tier 2 students may also receive a stipend for room/board/books subject to funding. Child must be 16-29; no age limit for spouse. Domicile requirements apply (Code of Va. 23.1-608).
value_description: Tuition + mandatory fees waived, up to 8 semesters; possible stipend

benefit_name: Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) Designation
description: Service-disabled veteran small business owners can obtain an SDVOSB designation in Virginia's SWaM vendor database, supporting state procurement preferences. Business must be at least 51% owned and both managed and operated by one or more service-disabled veterans.
value_description: State procurement designation

benefit_name: Veterans Hiring Preference (State & Local Government)
description: The Commonwealth and Virginia localities give hiring preference to honorably discharged veterans (and, where applicable, surviving spouses and children) who meet the job's knowledge, skill and eligibility requirements. For non-examination positions, qualified veterans receive preference in the selection process, with additional consideration for veterans who have a VA service-connected disability rating. Code of Va. 2.2-2903.
value_description: Hiring preference; added consideration for service-connected disability

benefit_name: Virginia Values Veterans (V3) Program
description: Free state training and certification program helping Virginia employers recruit, hire and retain veterans; also provides transition services to separating service members and veterans. Run by the Virginia Department of Veterans Services.
value_description: Free employer certification + veteran job placement support

benefit_name: Military Benefits Subtraction (Military Retirement / SBP)
description: Subtract up to $40,000 of military retirement income (and Survivor Benefit Plan payments to surviving spouses) from Virginia taxable income. No age requirement. Income must be reported on the federal return. Cap is $40,000 for tax year 2025 and after; beginning in 2026 the cap is indexed annually to CPI-U (never decreasing). Cannot be combined with another subtraction/deduction for the same income. — VERIFIED: Military retirement income is PARTIALLY exempt via Virginia's Military Benefits Subtraction: up to $40,000 of military retirement income (and SBP payments to surviving spouses) may be subtracted for taxable years beginning on/after Jan 1, 2025, with no age requirement. The research's core figure is right, but its claim that the $40,000 cap is 'indexed annually to CPI-U beginning in 2026' is NOT supported by the controlling statute (Code of Virginia 58.1-322.02, subdivision 18(c)), which sets a FLAT $40,000 with no inflation-adjustment/CPI mechanism. Correct status: partial exemption, flat $40,000 cap (not CPI-indexed).
value_description: Up to $40,000 subtraction (CPI-indexed from 2026)

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Not Taxed
description: VA disability compensation (and other qualified military benefits like DIC) is excluded from federal adjusted gross income, and therefore is never included in Virginia taxable income. Virginia does not tax VA disability pay.
value_description: Fully exempt (not in tax base)

benefit_name: Real Estate Tax Exemption for 100% Disabled Veterans
description: Full exemption from local real estate (property) tax on the veteran's principal residence and up to one acre of land (more if the locality exempts more acreage for others). Requires a VA rating of 100% service-connected, permanent and total disability (including 100% via Individual Unemployability that is also rated permanent and total). The non-remarried surviving spouse keeps the exemption if the veteran died on or after January 1, 2011. Filed via affidavit with the local commissioner of the revenue.
value_description: Full exemption on dwelling + 1 acre (or locality's larger acreage)

benefit_name: Resident Disabled Veteran Lifetime Hunting & Freshwater Fishing License
description: Lifetime hunting and freshwater fishing license for resident veterans with a service-connected disability of 30% or more. Fee is tiered by VA rating: Totally & Permanently disabled = $0 (free) for both hunting and freshwater fishing ($15 trapping); 70% or more = $50 each; 50% to less than 70% = $75 each; 30% to less than 50% = $100 each. Hunting license equals the small game license (includes archery, muzzleloader, bear, deer, turkey). Some trout/forest permits cost extra. Authorized by Code of Va. 29.1-302.
value_description: Free at Totally & Permanently disabled; $50/$75/$100 tiers at 70%/50%/30%

benefit_name: Free Disabled Veteran License Plates + Registration Fee Waiver
description: One set of Disabled Veteran special plates free, with annual registration fees waived, for a veteran who has lost or lost the use of a leg, arm or hand; is blind; or is rated permanent and total by the VA. Covers one personally owned/used vehicle. Personalization and emissions fees, if applicable, still apply.
value_description: Free plates + registration fee waiver (one vehicle)

benefit_name: Motor Vehicle Property (Personal Property) Tax Exemption
description: One motor vehicle owned and used primarily by or for a veteran rated 100% service-connected permanent and total by the VA is exempt from local personal property (car) tax. Established by Code of Virginia 58.1-3668.
value_description: Full exemption on one vehicle

benefit_name: Vehicle Sales and Use Tax (SUT) Exemption
description: Veterans of the U.S. Armed Forces or Virginia National Guard whom the VA has rated 100% service-connected permanent and total are exempt from Virginia motor vehicle Sales and Use Tax on the purchase of one vehicle used by or for the qualifying veteran.
value_description: Full SUT exemption on one vehicle purchase

benefit_name: Virginia Veterans Care Centers (State Veterans Homes / Skilled Nursing)
description: Virginia operates state veterans care centers providing skilled nursing and dementia/memory care to eligible veterans: Sitter & Barfoot (Richmond, 200 beds), Jones & Cabacoy (Virginia Beach, opened 2023), Davis & McDaniel (Roanoke), and Puller (Warrenton, opening late 2025). Operated by the Department of Veterans Services.
value_description: Skilled nursing / memory care at 4 state care centers

benefit_name: Free Tuition at University of the Virgin Islands (UVI) + DOE technical courses
description: FREE tuition is offered to Virgin Islands veterans for attendance at the University of the Virgin Islands (the veteran pays only fees). In addition, VI veterans may attend, without fee or charge of any kind, UVI CELL (Community Engagement & Lifelong Learning) courses and any academic or career-and-technical courses conducted by the V.I. Department of Education, day or night. Governed by OVA Policy #2. Applies to the narrowly defined 'Virgin Islands Veteran.'
value_description: Free tuition (fees paid by veteran); free UVI CELL and DOE technical courses

benefit_name: VI National Guard 100% Tuition Waiver (up to 32 semester hours)
description: Members of the USVI National Guard in good standing receive a 100% tuition waiver, not to exceed 32 semester hours, at USVI public educational institutions (associate through doctorate level). Distinct from the general veteran free-tuition benefit; tied to Guard service rather than VA rating.
value_description: 100% tuition waiver, up to 32 semester hours at USVI public institutions

benefit_name: Civil Service Veterans' Preference (10 / 15 points)
description: Veterans receive 10 points added to their score on local civil service examinations; disabled veterans receive 15 points. A 'disabled Veteran' for this preference is one with a service-connected disability currently rated at 0% or more. The widow or spouse of a disabled veteran is entitled to the same benefit.
value_description: +10 points (veteran); +15 points (disabled veteran, 0%+ rating)

benefit_name: Annual Taxi Medallion Auction (resident veterans only)
description: Resident veterans have the opportunity to acquire a Taxi Medallion through the annual VI Taxi Medallion auction (held in December), which is open only to resident veterans (OVA Policy #5). An economic/livelihood benefit rather than a cash grant.
value_description: Eligibility to bid on taxi medallions reserved for veterans

benefit_name: Burial Expense Reimbursement (up to $5,000)
description: A maximum of $5,000 is reimbursed for the burial expenses of a Virgin Islands veteran (governed by OVA Policy #4). Applies to the narrowly defined 'Virgin Islands Veteran' (entered service in the USVI or was a USVI domiciliary). Note: an older OVA flyer cited $3,500; the current Nov 19, 2024 Summary of Local Benefits states $5,000.
value_description: Up to $5,000 burial expense reimbursement

benefit_name: Veterans Home/Land Loan Program (up to $220,000 at 3.5%)
description: When funding is available, Virgin Islands veterans may obtain a home/land loan up to a maximum of $220,000 at a low rate of 3.5%. Subject to availability and to the narrow 'Virgin Islands Veteran' definition. Veterans at risk of foreclosure may also qualify for refinancing assistance.
value_description: Up to $220,000 at 3.5% (subject to availability)

benefit_name: Free Emergency Ambulance Service + Off-Island Medical Travel Reimbursement
description: FREE emergency ambulance service is provided to all veterans residing in the USVI. Additionally, 100% reimbursement of off-island travel is available for non-service-connected veterans attending a VA medical appointment at the nearest VA medical facility (governed by OVA Policy #3); travel within the VA Caribbean Network is reimbursed and out-of-network trips are reimbursed up to a per-trip cap.
value_description: Free emergency ambulance; 100% reimbursement of off-island VA medical travel (nearest facility)

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation — Not Taxed (mirror code)
description: The USVI income tax mirrors the federal Internal Revenue Code, administered by the V.I. Bureau of Internal Revenue. Because it mirrors the IRC, VA service-connected disability compensation is excluded from gross income (mirrored IRC §104) exactly as under federal law, so it is not subject to USVI income tax. Military retired pay, by contrast, is fully taxable.
value_description: VA disability compensation excluded from taxable income

benefit_name: Full Property Tax Exemption — Permanent & Total Disabled Veteran
description: Any veteran with a service-connected disability that the VA has determined entitles them to compensation for PERMANENT AND TOTAL disability is exempt from payment of ALL real property taxes levied against the disabled veteran's homestead. The widow/spouse of such a disabled veteran is entitled to the same benefit. Authorized under Title 33 of the V.I. Code (§2305) and administered via OVA Policy Letter #6 — the veteran must obtain a Veteran Tax Exemption Eligibility Form signed by the OVA Director. This is a FULL exemption, not a capped credit.
value_description: Full exemption of all real property taxes on the homestead

benefit_name: Veteran Homestead Tax Credit ($650)
description: A $650 tax credit applied to the real property tax bill of a property owner who is an honorably discharged veteran (or the widow/widower of a veteran) of the U.S. Armed Forces and who occupies the property as their primary homestead. No VA disability rating is required — only an other-than-dishonorable discharge (DD-214) and proof of USVI residency/occupancy. Claimed via the annual Homestead Tax Credit application (deadline ~March 1) filed with the Office of the Lieutenant Governor/Tax Assessor. Confirmed verbatim ('A Veteran tax credit of $650') on the official USVI homestead application.
value_description: $650 credit against real property tax bill

benefit_name: "Veteran" Designation on Driver's License / State ID
description: Eligible veterans (including VI National Guard retirees with 20+ years) may have a 'Veteran' designation added to their driver's license or territorial ID card issued by the Bureau of Motor Vehicles (BMV).
value_description: Veteran designation on driver's license or ID

benefit_name: Free Veteran License Plates (up to 3) + Disabled Veteran Plate
description: Up to 3 FREE Veteran license plates are available to eligible veterans (registration fees themselves must be paid by the veteran).
