A special Disabled License Plate is available to veterans rated PERMANENT AND TOTAL disabled based on military service, documented on the VA Benefit Summary Letter — only one disabled plate per eligible veteran. Issued through the Bureau of Motor Vehicles.
value_description: Up to 3 free veteran plates; 1 disabled-veteran plate for P&T disabled (registration fees still apply)

benefit_name: No State Veterans Home (federal VA Caribbean care instead)
description: The U.S. Virgin Islands does NOT operate a territorial/state veterans home or skilled nursing facility. USVI veterans needing nursing/long-term care are served through the federal VA Caribbean Healthcare System — the San Juan VA Medical Center (Puerto Rico) operates the nursing-home beds, with community-based outpatient clinics on St. Croix and St. Thomas. Listed here to explicitly document the absence of a state home.
value_description: None — no territorial veterans home exists; care via federal VA Caribbean system

benefit_name: Vermont Armed Services Scholarship
description: Scholarship for the children and spouses of Vermont service members who were killed in the line of duty (service-connected death) after 2001. Administered through the state. Helps cover the cost of attending a Vermont postsecondary institution.

benefit_name: Vermont National Guard Tuition Benefit Program (16 V.S.A. § 2857)
description: Interest-free, forgivable loan that pays up to 100% of tuition for active members in good standing of the Vermont National Guard who have completed basic training. Covers up to full tuition at Vermont State University or UVM, or up to UVM's tuition rate at a Vermont private or out-of-state school. Administered by VSAC. Covers tuition only (not fees, room, or board). Requires Guard membership, not a disability rating.
value_description: Up to 100% of tuition (interest-free forgivable loan)

benefit_name: State Civil Service Veterans Hiring Preference
description: Veterans receive a preference in state civil service hiring, applied as added points to competitive examination/scoring. State employees who are members of a reserve component are also authorized up to 15 days of paid military leave per year for required training without loss of pay. Job counseling and training assistance is available through the Vermont Department of Labor.
value_description: Points added to civil service exam scores; up to 15 days paid military leave

benefit_name: Veteran Assistance Fund (Emergency Financial Assistance)
description: One-time emergency cash assistance administered by the Vermont Office of Veterans Affairs for Vermont-resident veterans and their families in financial crisis with a critical need such as housing or utilities. Applications are taken by phone (about 10 minutes) by calling the Office of Veterans Affairs. No disability rating required.
value_description: Up to $500, one-time payment

benefit_name: Low-Income Veteran Refundable Tax Credit (Act 71 of 2025)
description: Refundable $250 income tax credit for any Vermont resident or part-year resident with a discharge/separation record verifying uniformed service, whose AGI is at or below $25,000. The credit phases down for AGI between $25,000 and $30,000 and is unavailable at AGI above $30,000. Effective 2025 tax year. Does not require a disability rating.
value_description: $250 refundable credit (AGI <= $25,000; phases out by $30,000)

benefit_name: Military Retirement & Survivor Benefit Income Exemption (Act 71 of 2025)
description: Income-tiered exemption effective beginning the 2025 tax year (claimed when filing in 2026). Military retirement pay and survivor benefit income are fully exempt from Vermont income tax if AGI is at or below $125,000; the exemption phases down for AGI between $125,000 and $175,000; no exemption at $175,000 or above. Applies to any filing status. This is a major change from Vermont's prior full taxation of military retirement.
value_description: Full exemption if AGI <= $125,000; phased-down $125K-$175K; none at $175K+

benefit_name: National Guard / Reserve Drill Pay Exclusion
description: Vermont allows up to $2,000 of National Guard and Reserve pay (for unit training assemblies/drill) to be excluded from Vermont income, available if federal AGI is under $50,000. Active duty pay earned while serving outside Vermont is also exempt.
value_description: Up to $2,000 excluded (federal AGI under $50,000)

benefit_name: Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption (32 V.S.A. § 3802(11))
description: Reduces the assessed value of a disabled veteran's primary residence (fee-simple owned) before tax is calculated. Eligible if the veteran has a VA service-connected disability rating of 50% or higher, OR receives a non-service-connected VA pension (improved pension), OR has a permanent medical military retirement. State law mandates a minimum exemption; towns may vote to increase it. Available to unremarried surviving spouses and minor children of qualifying veterans. Application filed with the Vermont Office of Veterans Affairs by May 1; for permanent disability it is filed once and remains on the grand list until the property is transferred.
value_description: Minimum $10,000 of appraised value exempt; municipality may vote to increase up to $40,000

benefit_name: Free Permanent Hunting / Fishing / Combination License (Disabled Veteran)
description: Vermont-resident veterans with a VA service-connected disability rating of 60% or greater qualify for a free permanent fishing, hunting, or combination license. Maine and New Hampshire resident veterans with a 60%+ rating qualify for a free one-year reciprocal Vermont license. Active duty/reserve members on 120+ days of activation receive a free one-year license. Apply to the Vermont Fish & Wildlife Department with a VA benefits-summary verification letter.
value_description: Free permanent license

benefit_name: Green Mountain Passport (State Parks & Historic Sites)
description: Lifetime pass giving free day-use entry to Vermont State Parks and Vermont State-owned Historic Sites. Available to any Vermont resident veteran who was honorably discharged from active duty in the U.S. armed services (also available to residents age 62+). Purchased for a one-time $2 fee through the applicant's local town clerk. No disability rating required.
value_description: $2 lifetime pass (free state-park day-use entry)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran & Military License Plates
description: Vermont DMV issues a Disabled Veteran license plate to veterans with a service-connected disability, plus a range of military/veteran specialty plates (U.S. Veteran, Purple Heart, Gold Star Family, etc.). The 'VETERAN' designation can also be added to a Vermont driver's license or ID card with proof of honorable service. Plates indicate status; the specialty plate itself does not by itself waive registration fees (see separate adapted-vehicle exemption).

benefit_name: Registration / Sales Tax / License Fee Exemption for VA-Adapted Vehicles
description: NARROW benefit: A disabled veteran pays no vehicle sales/use tax, no registration fee, and no license fee ONLY where the vehicle was modified to accommodate the disability and the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs paid for the modification. This is NOT a general exemption for disabled veterans; even 100% disabled veterans without a VA-funded vehicle adaptation are not exempt. Requires Form VG-168 (Vermont Certificate of Veteran Status, certified by the Office of Veterans Affairs) plus medical certification.
value_description: Full waiver of sales/use tax, registration fee, and license fee (only for VA-funded adapted vehicles)

benefit_name: Vermont Veterans Memorial Cemetery (Randolph Center)
description: State veterans cemetery offering burial at no cost for eligible veterans, including a government grave marker. Opening/closing (interment) fees apply for eligible spouses and dependent children. Military funeral honors are available through the Vermont National Guard.
value_description: Free burial for eligible veterans; fees for dependents

benefit_name: Vermont Veterans' Home (Bennington)
description: State-operated long-term care facility in Bennington providing skilled nursing, rehabilitation, and long-term care to eligible Vermont veterans (and in some cases spouses and Gold Star parents). Cost is offset by VA per-diem and other benefits; admission is based on eligibility and need.

benefit_name: Washington Tuition Waiver for Dependents of Totally Disabled / Deceased Veterans (RCW 28B.15.621)
description: State community/technical colleges, colleges and universities must waive all undergraduate tuition and fees (up to 200 quarter credits or equivalent) for eligible children (ages 17-26, WA domiciliary) and surviving spouses/domestic partners of a veteran or National Guard member who died, became totally disabled, or is a POW/MIA as a result of qualifying service. Recipients also receive a textbook/course-materials stipend of $500 per academic year when funded by the legislature. Each institution runs its own program — contact the school's veterans office.
value_description: Full undergraduate tuition + fee waiver (up to 200 quarter credits) + $500/yr book stipend

benefit_name: Veterans' Scoring Criteria (Hiring Preference) — RCW 41.04.010
description: In all competitive civil-service examinations for state and local public employment, veterans receive points added to a passing score: 10% for a veteran who served during a period of war/armed conflict and is not receiving military retirement (until first appointment); 5% for a veteran who did not serve during war/armed conflict or who is receiving military retirement (until first appointment); and 5% added to promotional exams for a veteran called to active service from a state/local government job (first promotion only). Points are only added to an already-passing score and require a qualifying discharge.
value_description: 10% or 5% added to passing civil-service exam scores

benefit_name: County Veterans Assistance Fund (emergency relief)
description: Washington counties maintain Veterans Assistance Funds providing emergency financial relief (rent, utilities, food, etc.) to qualifying low-income veterans. Typical qualifications: WA resident 12+ consecutive months, resident of the county at application, and household income within 200% of federal poverty guidelines. Administered at the county level — amounts and exact rules vary by county; not a statewide fixed benefit.
value_description: Emergency cash/in-kind relief (varies by county; income ≤200% FPL typical)

benefit_name: Veterans Estate Management Program (VEMP)
description: WDVA program providing protective-payee / fiduciary financial-management services for veterans and family members who cannot manage their own finances, ensuring housing, food, clothing, and medical needs are paid. Includes VA-appointed fiduciary and SSA representative-payee services. Not a cash grant; it is a money-management protective service.
value_description: Protective payee / fiduciary money-management service (not a cash grant)

benefit_name: No State Income Tax (military retirement & VA disability untaxed)
description: Washington levies no personal income tax, so military retirement pay and VA disability compensation are not taxed at the state level for any veteran or retiree. There is no state filing for these income types.
value_description: Military retirement fully untaxed; VA disability untaxed (no state income tax exists)

benefit_name: Property Tax Exemption — DVA program overview page
description: WDVA's veteran-facing summary of the property tax relief program. As of this page's text it still cites the 80% (or 100%-rate) threshold and explains the income test and that VA disability compensation is excluded from disposable income; the statutory threshold has since been lowered to 40% by EHB 1106 (see DOR source). Confirms the program freezes value and exempts excess/special levies and is administered by county assessors.
value_description: See RCW 84.36.381 program; income-based

benefit_name: Senior Citizen & Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption (RCW 84.36.381)
description: Income-based property tax exemption for disabled veterans. Qualifying veterans get the assessed value of their residence frozen and are exempted from all excess (voter-approved) and special levies, and may also be exempt from a portion of regular levies depending on income tier. Veteran must own and occupy the home and meet a county-specific 'combined disposable income' threshold (based on each county's median household income); VA disability compensation and DIC are EXCLUDED from that income calculation. CRITICAL 2025 CHANGE: Engrossed House Bill 1106 (signed by Gov. Ferguson May 7, 2025; effective July 27, 2025) lowers the qualifying VA service-connected rating from 80% to a phased schedule — 60% for taxes levied for collection in 2026, and 40% for taxes levied for collection in 2027 and after. Veterans rated 100% (or paid at the 100% rate) have always qualified. Apply through the local county assessor. — VERIFIED: Income-based Senior/Disabled/Veterans Property Tax Exemption (RCW 84.36.381, the SPTE program): qualifying disabled veterans get a frozen (non-increasing) assessed valuation plus a partial exemption scaled by income tier — full exemption from all excess (voter-approved) levies and the additional state school levy at every tier, plus a reduction of regular levies for the two lower income tiers. VA disability compensation/DIC is excluded from 'combined disposable income.' Rating threshold per EHB 1106 (As Passed Legislature, signed 2025): a SINGLE reduction from 80% to 40% (or paid at the 100% rate), effective for taxes levied for collection in calendar year 2027 and thereafter — there is NO phased 60%-for-2026 step. For taxes levied for collection in 2026 the threshold remains 80% (or the 100% rate); 40% applies beginning with 2027 collections. The research's min_rating_pct of 40 with a '60% for 2026 / 40% for 2027' phase-in is incorrect: the Senate amended out the 60% step, so 80% still governs 2026 collections.
value_description: Frozen assessed value + full exemption from excess/special levies (income-based)

benefit_name: Lifetime Disabled Veteran Pass (State Parks)
description: Washington-resident veterans with a 30% or greater combined service-connected disability rating qualify for a free Lifetime Disabled Veteran Pass from Washington State Parks. It provides free day-use parking (no Discover Pass needed), free camping or moorage, and free boat launch/trailer dump at state parks, plus free day-use parking and Discover Pass privileges on WA Dept. of Fish & Wildlife and Dept. of Natural Resources lands.
value_description: Free lifetime pass: free day-use parking, camping/moorage, boat launch

benefit_name: WDFW Reduced-Fee Veteran Hunting & Fishing Licenses
description: The Washington Dept. of Fish & Wildlife offers reduced-fee hunting and fishing licenses to resident veterans with a VA service-connected disability rating of at least 30% (verified by VA letter), and to resident veterans age 65 or older with a service-connected disability. Applicants must show WA residency and a VA award letter. The specific discounted fee amounts vary by license type — see the WDFW fee schedule.
value_description: Discounted (reduced-fee) hunting/fishing licenses; exact $ varies by license

benefit_name: Disabled American Veteran (DAV) License Plate + Registration Fee Exemption (RCW 46.18.235)
description: Veterans with a 100% service-connected disability rating expected to last more than one year (or who have loss of use of both hands/one foot, or blindness in both eyes from service) may obtain Disabled American Veteran specialty plates. The plates are exempt from license/registration fees on one vehicle (personal-use passenger vehicle, truck, motorcycle, or motor home) per year and are good for the life of the veteran. Issued by the Department of Licensing (DOL).
value_description: Free registration/license fees on 1 personal-use vehicle per year, for life

benefit_name: Washington State Veterans Homes (4 skilled-nursing facilities)
description: WDVA operates four state veterans homes providing 24-hour skilled nursing, rehabilitation, and long-term care: Washington Soldiers Home (Orting), Washington Veterans Home–Retsil (Port Orchard), Spokane Veterans Home, and Walla Walla Veterans Home. All are Medicare- and Medicaid-certified. Open to eligible veterans (and in some cases spouses/Gold Star parents); admission via the WDVA admissions team. Cost is based on ability to pay / VA per-diem and Medicare/Medicaid coverage — there is no fixed published fee.
value_description: Subsidized long-term skilled nursing care (cost based on ability to pay / VA per diem)

benefit_name: Wisconsin G.I. Bill (Tuition Remission)
description: Remission of tuition and fees at Universities of Wisconsin (UW System) and Wisconsin Technical College System (WTCS) schools for eligible Wisconsin veterans and certain spouses and children. Veterans qualify if they were Wisconsin residents at entry to service or lived in Wisconsin for 5 consecutive years; spouses and children (ages 17-25/26) of eligible veterans also qualify. Benefit covers up to 128 credits or 8 semesters (whichever is greater); student must maintain 2.0 GPA and first apply any federal Post-9/11 GI Bill benefits. WDVA verifies eligibility.
value_description: Full tuition and fee remission, up to 128 credits / 8 semesters

benefit_name: Veterans Preference & Non-Competitive Appointment (Ch. 230)
description: Wisconsin civil service grants veterans preference under Wis. Stat. ch. 230: additional points on civil-service scores for veterans and certain spouses; expanded certification of the 3 highest-ranked disabled veterans whose disability is at least 70% (and spouses of such veterans); and a non-competitive appointment pathway allowing veterans with a service-connected disability rating of 30% or more to be hired into state agency jobs without competing or taking the assessment. State agencies also have statutory veteran-hiring goals.
value_description: Preference points; non-competitive appointment at 30%+; expanded certification at 70%+

benefit_name: Health Care Aid Grant (dental / vision / hearing)
description: Limited financial assistance toward dental care (including dentures), vision care (eyeglasses), and hearing care (hearing aids) for needy veterans. Income limit is under 200% of the federal poverty level, and the health care component is further limited to veterans with liquid assets below $1,000 (excluding the first $50,000 of life insurance cash surrender value).
value_description: Aid for dental/vision/hearing costs (income under 200% FPL; liquid assets under $1,000)

benefit_name: Subsistence Aid Grant
description: Limited financial assistance for needy veterans who have exhausted other aid. Eligibility limited to veterans in families with income under 200% of the federal poverty level. Maximum subsistence aid payable in a consecutive 12-month period is $3,000. Applied for through a county/tribal veterans service officer or WDVA (form WDVA 2453).
value_description: Up to $3,000 in a 12-month period (income under 200% FPL)

benefit_name: Military Retirement Pay Exemption
description: All U.S. military retirement system payments (including SBP and Retired Serviceman's Family Protection Plan) are fully exempt from Wisconsin income tax, subtracted on Schedule SB line 12. Coast Guard, NOAA commissioned corps, and Public Health Service commissioned corps retirement payments are also exempt.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Exclusion
description: VA service-connected disability compensation is not taxable income in Wisconsin (it is excluded from federal and therefore Wisconsin gross income). Applies to all rated veterans regardless of percentage.
value_description: VA disability compensation not taxed

benefit_name: Veterans and Surviving Spouses Property Tax Credit
description: Refundable state income-tax credit equal to the property taxes paid on the veteran's (or unremarried surviving spouse's) principal Wisconsin dwelling. Under CURRENT operative law the veteran must have a service-connected disability rating of 100% (or 100% based on individual unemployability) and meet Wisconsin residency rules; WDVA verifies eligibility and the credit is claimed on the Wisconsin income tax return. NOTE: 2025 SB411/AB428 would lower the threshold to 80% and prorate the credit by rating, but as of mid-2026 those are still pending proposals, not enacted law - the DOR FAQ still states 100%.
value_description: Credit equal to property taxes paid on principal dwelling (refundable); no income limit

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran / Former POW State Park Admission Card
description: Free state park vehicle admission and trail pass (Park Admission Card) for veterans with a service-connected disability rating of at least 70% or former POWs who are Wisconsin residents. Issued for 2 years or lifetime. Application must be certified by the County Veteran Service Officer.
value_description: Free park admission / trail pass (70%+ rating or former POW)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Fishing License
description: Reduced-cost annual resident fishing license for veterans with a service-connected disability rating of at least 70%. Cost is $3 per license year.
value_description: $3 annual fishing license (70%+ rating)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Recreation Card
description: Reduced-cost annual Recreation Card for veterans with a service-connected disability rating of at least 50%. Cost is $7; includes fishing, small game, vehicle park admission, heritage park admission, and a state trail pass.
value_description: $7 annual Recreation Card (50%+ rating)

benefit_name: Returning Veteran One-Time Free License
description: Recently returning Wisconsin resident veterans receive a one-time free choice of a small game license, archery license, gun deer license, or annual fishing license (requires a voucher from WDVA). Authorized by 2013 Wisconsin Act 20.
value_description: One free hunting or fishing license (one-time)

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran (VET) Parking License Plates
description: Veterans with U.S. military service that limits or impairs the ability to walk may obtain Disabled Veteran parking license plates. The vehicle must be titled to the disabled veteran; application MV2172 requires VA health care certification. These plates confer disabled-parking privileges.
value_description: Disabled veteran parking plates

benefit_name: WDVA Initial License Fee Waiver Program (Act 209)
description: Under 2017 Wisconsin Act 209, WisDOT (and other state licensing agencies) waive fees for qualified veterans on issuance of an INITIAL license. Veteran must be a Wisconsin resident and honorably discharged; WDVA determines eligibility. Waivers are not allowed for renewals - one initial license only. This is a professional/occupational/DMV initial-license fee waiver, not a blanket vehicle registration waiver.
value_description: Waiver of initial license fee (one-time, no renewals)

benefit_name: Wisconsin Veterans Homes (King, Union Grove, Chippewa Falls)
description: WDVA operates three state veterans homes - King (N2665 County Rd QQ, King), Union Grove, and Chippewa Falls - serving nearly 1,000 veterans and eligible spouses. Services include 24-hour skilled nursing care, assisted living, and protective memory care. Eligibility is determined by WDVA.
value_description: Skilled nursing / assisted living / memory care at 3 state homes

benefit_name: In-State Tuition for GI Bill Recipients
description: All West Virginia public higher education institutions charge the in-state tuition rate to nonresident GI Bill recipients who enroll within three years of discharge (effective July 2015). Institutions also participate in the VA Yellow Ribbon Program.
value_description: In-state tuition rate

benefit_name: Medal of Honor / Purple Heart Tuition Waiver
description: All West Virginia public institutions of higher education waive tuition and mandatory fee charges for WV resident veterans who were awarded the Medal of Honor or the Purple Heart.
value_description: Full tuition and mandatory fee waiver

benefit_name: Veterans Re-education Assistance (Education Stipend)
description: State-funded program providing up to a $500 per semester stipend to West Virginia veterans enrolled in a certified post-secondary education course. May also assist with vocational and non-traditional education on a case-by-case basis. Administered through the WV Department of Veterans Assistance.
value_description: Up to $500 per semester

benefit_name: War Orphan Education Program
description: Children (under age 26) and surviving spouses of qualifying deceased/disabled veterans receive a waiver of tuition and fees at West Virginia state post-secondary institutions, plus up to $1,000 per semester (maximum $2,000 per year) for educational expenses.
value_description: Tuition/fee waiver plus up to $1,000/semester ($2,000/year)

benefit_name: Veterans Preference Points (State Civil Service)
description: Under W. Va. Code §6-13, qualifying veterans receive a 5-point preference added to a passing state employment examination score. Disabled veterans (meeting the 'disabled veteran' definition / compensable service-connected disability) receive a 10-point preference. DD-214 documentation required. Administered by the WV Division of Personnel.
value_description: 5 points (veteran) / 10 points (disabled veteran)

benefit_name: Military Retirement Income Exemption
description: All military retirement income (regular Armed Forces, Reserves, National Guard, and uniformed services such as PHS and NOAA), including survivorship annuities, is fully exempt from West Virginia personal income tax as a decreasing modification to federal AGI for tax years beginning after Dec 31, 2017/2018. The earlier $20,000/$30,000 caps were repealed; it is now 100% exempt.
value_description: Military retirement fully tax-exempt (no cap)

benefit_name: VA Disability Compensation Not Taxed
description: VA disability compensation is not subject to West Virginia income tax. West Virginia uses federal adjusted gross income as its starting point, and VA disability compensation is excluded from federal gross income, so it is never taxed at the state level. No special state modification is needed.
value_description: Not taxable

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Exemption
description: Veterans rated 100% permanent and total (P&T) disabled by the VA may be exempt from certain ad valorem property taxes on their homestead. Administered through the county assessor under W. Va. Code §11-6B. The WV Veterans Affairs page states the requirement as '100 percent permanent and disabled by the federal Department of Veterans Affairs.' — VERIFIED: The current (2024+) West Virginia disabled-veteran property benefit is the Disabled Veteran Real Property Tax Credit, NOT the old 100% P&T flat homestead exemption that the research leads with. Per WV Tax Division publication TSD 455 (Rev. January 2026): honorably discharged veterans considered 90-100% permanently and totally (P&T) disabled by the VA may claim a credit equal to the ACTUAL real property tax paid on a homestead used/occupied exclusively for residential purposes (used or occupied at least 6 months). The credit is claimed on Form DV-1 / IT-140 Line 21(B); property taxes must be paid timely (first half before Oct 1, second half before Apr 1). Threshold is 90-100% P&T (P&T required), and the extent is the full property tax actually paid (a refundable income-tax credit), not a $20,000 partial assessed-value exemption. The research's second property item (the 90% credit) is essentially correct; its first item (100% P&T flat homestead exemption as the headline benefit) is outdated/superseded for veterans as of Jan 1, 2024.
value_description: Exemption from certain property taxes

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran Real Property Tax Credit (Income Tax Credit)
description: Honorably discharged veterans with a 90-100% combined VA disability rating may claim a refundable state income tax credit equal to the property tax timely paid on their West Virginia homestead (single-family residence used/occupied exclusively for residential purposes). The credit is claimed on the state income tax return. First available on 2024 returns filed in 2025. Property taxes must be paid on time (first half by Oct 1, second half by Apr 1).
value_description: Credit equal to property tax paid on homestead

benefit_name: Free Hunting and Fishing License
description: Free hunting and fishing licenses for West Virginia resident veterans with a 100% permanent and total disability rating, those who qualify for the federal VA automobile grant, and former POWs. Active duty service members on leave/furlough may hunt and fish without a license.
value_description: Free hunting/fishing license

benefit_name: State Parks Lodging/Camping Discount
description: Veterans and active-duty military receive a 10% discount on all standard lodge rooms, cabins, and campsites at West Virginia state parks, year-round.
value_description: 10% discount on lodge rooms, cabins, and campsites

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran / Special Veteran License Plates
description: Special license plates are available at no fee to veterans with a 100% permanent and total service-connected disability rating or who qualify for the federal VA automobile grant, as well as to former POWs, Purple Heart recipients, and Pearl Harbor survivors. Other honorably discharged veterans may obtain a veteran plate for $10 over the regular registration fee.
value_description: Free for 100% P&T / VA auto grant; $10 surcharge for general veteran plates

benefit_name: West Virginia Veterans Home (Barboursville)
description: State-operated 125-bed facility in Barboursville providing residential, nursing, and rehabilitative care to elderly and chronically ill West Virginia veterans and eligible family members. Authorized by the Legislature in 1975, opened 1981. Operated by the WV Department of Veterans Assistance.
value_description: 125-bed residential/nursing/rehab facility

benefit_name: Overseas Combat Veteran / Survivors & Dependents Tuition Benefit (W.S. 19-14-106)
description: Free tuition (and, for those enrolled before summer 2018, mandatory fees) at the University of Wyoming and Wyoming community colleges for overseas combat veterans, and for surviving spouses and dependents of veterans who died in active service. Capped at 8 semesters within an 8-year period for participants from summer 2018 forward (older enrollees had up to 10 semesters). Tuition-only for the 2018-forward cohort; fees no longer covered for them.
value_description: Tuition (and fees for pre-2018 enrollees) waived, up to 8 semesters over 8 years

benefit_name: Veterans Interview Preference in Public Employment (W.S. 19-14-102)
description: In every Wyoming public department (state, counties, municipalities, school districts, special districts, community colleges, and the University of Wyoming), qualified veterans and their surviving spouses receive a preference prior to the interview process. Disabled veterans whose disabilities do not materially interfere with the duties are given preference over able-bodied veterans. No preference applies to a veteran already employed by that public department.
value_description: Guaranteed interview preference (not a points system)

benefit_name: No State Income Tax (military retirement & VA disability fully untaxed)
description: Wyoming imposes no individual income tax. Military retirement pay, VA disability compensation, wages, Social Security, and investment income are all free of state income tax because the state has no income tax at all.
value_description: Military retirement and VA disability fully exempt (no state income tax)

benefit_name: Veterans Property Tax Exemption (W.S. 39-13-105)
description: Reduces the assessed value of a qualifying veteran's primary residence (or, at the veteran's option, can be applied to motor-vehicle registration fees). Qualifying veterans include honorably discharged wartime/expeditionary-medal veterans (WWI, WWII, Korea, Vietnam, or a foreign-conflict expeditionary/campaign medal), their unremarried surviving spouses, AND any veteran with a compensable (10%+) service-connected disability certified by the VA. Disabled veterans qualify regardless of combat service. Applicant must be a current Wyoming resident and have lived in the state at least three years; must re-apply/confirm annually with the county assessor. Deadline is the fourth Monday in May.
value_description: $6,000 reduction in assessed value (approx. $350 in tax relief beginning tax year 2025, up from ~$175, per 2024 legislation). NOTE: the disabled-veteran path requires only a compensable rating (10%+), NOT 100%/P&T.

benefit_name: Free Hunting/Fishing Licenses for Disabled & Purple Heart Veterans (Game & Fish)
description: Wyoming Game & Fish issues free lifetime licenses to resident veterans: (1) 100% VA-disabled honorably discharged residents get free lifetime game bird, small game, and fishing licenses; (2) residents with a 50%+ disability get a free lifetime fishing license; (3) Purple Heart recipients (resident) get free lifetime game bird, small game, and fishing licenses regardless of rating; (4) resident active-duty members in combat zones may receive free general elk/deer, bird/small game, and daily fishing licenses. Veterans hospitalized in a VA hospital in Wyoming may fish free while under the hospital's control.
value_description: Free lifetime licenses: 100% = bird/small game/fish; 50%+ = fishing; Purple Heart = bird/small game/fish

benefit_name: Disabled Veteran License Plate
description: Wyoming issues a Disabled Veteran specialty plate to veterans with a 50% or greater service-connected disability rating from the VA. Apply via Form MV-100 through the county treasurer. Note: per WYDOT, the disabled-veteran plate itself does NOT exempt the standard registration fee, and it does not authorize handicap parking (a separate placard is required). Some county/military-benefit summaries describe a registration-fee exemption tied to the property-tax exemption being applied to vehicle registration instead of real property; confirm fee treatment with the county treasurer.
value_description: Disabled veteran plate at 50%+ rating; fee exemption not confirmed on WYDOT page

benefit_name: Veterans' Home of Wyoming (Buffalo)
description: State veterans home in Buffalo, WY operated under the Wyoming Department of Health, offering an Assisted Living (domiciliary) Community and a Skilled Nursing Community (36-bed 'Greenhouse'-model facility opened Oct 2022). Open to veterans, their spouses, surviving spouses, and Gold Star Family members who qualify for the level of care. No specific VA disability rating is published as a precondition; eligibility is care-need and service based (verify with admissions).
value_description: Assisted living + skilled nursing for veterans, spouses, surviving spouses, Gold Star Families
